source.notes: Shared component registry compiled from the design-system page and React implementation. Tool templates decide where each component can be used.

observedStructure.logoSlot: 208x60, x=28, y=10, centered inside the header area
observedStructure.rule: This component reserves the game logo image area. It does not render a visible text title in the map template.

logoSrc.rule: User-provided game logo image. Do not invent or substitute a fake logo.

title.rule: Used only for accessibility/alt text if available; not rendered as visible title text in the map header.

observedStructure.templateInstance: x=252, y=102, w=12, h=586 inside Panel Content
observedStructure.track: 12px wide, transparent track, no panel-background block. Use 8px top/bottom track padding unless the template explicitly overrides it.
observedStructure.thumb: 4px wide, 80px high by default, x=4, radius 999, fill #FFFFFF @ 12%. Thumb y = trackPadding + scrollRatio * (trackHeight - thumbHeight - trackPadding * 2).
observedStructure.rule: Use as a visual scrollbar that reflects renderer scroll state. It does not define datasource fields. Track height comes from the rendered scrollbar instance or template position, not automatically from the content viewport unless they are intentionally the same.

trackHeight.rule: Visual scrollbar track height. Use the actual Layout.Scroll instance height from the template. If omitted, the renderer may fall back to viewportHeight only when the scrollbar track exactly matches the viewport.

trackPadding.rule: Top and bottom inset for the thumb movement area.

visible.rule: Renderer may hide it when list content does not overflow.

thumbHeight.rule: Keep the same scroll behavior as the map component. Position and height should match the source page or observed template state for the current Wiki detail page.

typography.label: Tokenized Body/MD; use --type-font-family-primary, --type-size-200, --type-line-height-200. Prefer mobile-specific type token when present.

disabled.rule: When true, render state=Disabled and do not open DropdownCard. Renderer may also disable Select when the datasource has only one map and uiRules.singleMapSelect requests disabled behavior.

states.disabled: background #2C3134, stroke #43484B, text #ECEEF0, arrow #696E72; no dropdown interaction

observedStructure.mobile: 343x42 in BottomSheet, x=16, y=28, background var(--color-bg-soft), stroke var(--color-stroke-sub)

typography.input: Tokenized Body/MD; use --type-font-family-primary, --type-size-200, --type-line-height-200. Prefer mobile-specific type token when present.
typography.placeholder: Default color #AEB4B8; hover color #ECEEF0; active text color #FFFFFF.

states.active: opacity 80%, background #2C3134, 1px #DCB325 stroke, empty focused input, clear icon hidden
states.filled: opacity 80%, background #2C3134, 1px #DCB325 stroke, filled input text, right close-circle-fill icon visible

observedStructure.mobile: 343x36 mobile search overlay at x=16, y=52 inside MapCollectionModal; not a BottomSheet child and not clipped by BottomSheet height.
observedStructure.mobileFocused: Search-focused state keeps SearchBar overlay x=16, y=52, w=343, h=36 while BottomSheet is expanded to x=0, y=68, w=375, h=652; footer omitted in observed state.
observedStructure.mobileCollapsed: In the fully collapsed mobile sheet state, SearchBar remains as MapCollectionModal overlay at x=16, y=52, w=343, h=36; do not move it into the 100px BottomSheet.
observedStructure.mobileMarkerSelected: When a marker is selected, SearchBar remains visible as MapCollectionModal overlay at x=16, y=52, w=343, h=36; BottomSheet is hidden and Map.PopupDescribeCard appears at x=16, y=602, w=343, h=86.

value.rule: In Wiki shell usage, this is the global Wiki search query across all Wiki categories and detail entries.

onClear.rule: Clear button appears only when value is non-empty.

interactionState.rule: Mirrors Figma interactionState variants; disabled may also be derived from the disabled prop.

typography.label: Tokenized Body/MD, 14px/20px or mobile-specific text token when present

count.rule: required for child/item rows when source provides counts; parent rows do not need count

observedStructure.mobile: 343x36 row in SheetItemList; child rows use 32px left padding, optional 20px icon, optional count badge.

widthBehavior.rule: When used as Form.Select dropdown content, width must match the Select trigger width.

selected.rule: Selected option shows a right-side check-line icon.

props.rule: Only render between expanded DropdownItem content and the next parent item in the sidebar.

label.rule: Render only approved badge text from datasource or routing context. Do not invent placeholder labels.

tone.rule: Use source-provided tag type when available. For Wiki attribute tags, assign the same tone to the same tag class and a different stable tone to different tag classes when source colors are not provided.

variant.rule: Use variant=counter when the badge content should be changed by adjacent decrement/increment controls instead of rendering as a static label.

onDecrement.rule: Used only for variant=counter. Clicking the left control should switch to the previous badge value or decrement a numeric value.

onIncrement.rule: Used only for variant=counter. Clicking the right control should switch to the next badge value or increment a numeric value.

observedStructure.mobile: count badge at row end, uses --color-bg-alpha-weak and Label/MD tokens
observedStructure.counter: Counter variant is a horizontal group: 22x22 subtract button, center badge content, 22x22 add button, with 4px gap between items.

state.rule: Used by preview and visual-state rendering; runtime interaction may also use CSS pseudo states and disabled.

observedStructure.mobileSearchFocused: Footer/button may be omitted in MobileMapSearchFocusedScreen while search is focused.
observedStructure.mobileMarkerSelected: Footer/button and BottomSheet are hidden while selected marker detail card is open.

observedStructure.container: Shared Wiki header shell at x=0, y=0, w=1000, h=84. It contains the global TopBar and global SearchBar.
observedStructure.padding: Desktop header padding is top=32, right=32, bottom=16, left=32. The bottom 16px is part of the header mask/scroll cutoff area.
observedStructure.layout: TopBar and SearchBar stay aligned at y=32 with height 36. Other page content starts after the 84px header region and should disappear under the header from its bottom padding boundary while scrolling.
observedStructure.rule: Do not place page body content inside the header padding. Template body/content absolute positions should shift down with the 84px header.
observedStructure.mobileRule: Below 600px, do not reuse desktop Nav.Header. Use the host/product mobile shell behavior from the Wiki mobile template reference: MobileHeader/TitlebarCenter x=0, y=0, w=375, h=92 with content starting at y=104. This mobile header is not a formal component export in the current package, and it must not replace the page's product-customized body structure.

children.rule: When provided, replace the default TopBar content.

observedStructure.container: Global first-level Wiki navigation container. Background uses color/bg/alpha-bg equivalent alpha surface and a 6px background blur/backdrop-filter from Figma node 312:3809.
observedStructure.layout: Horizontal flex row, 8px item gap, radius md, 8 items observed at w=80, h=36.
observedStructure.rule: Keep the blur on the TopBar container only. Do not add blur to individual TopBarItem states.
observedStructure.mobileMenuRule: Below 600px, Nav.TopBar items are not rendered as the desktop header strip. The right header menu opens the mobile primary navigation panel from Figma node 590:9324 and reuses the same items and activeId datasource. This is navigation chrome only; do not use the mobile menu reference to replace or reorder the page's body content.

items.rule: Global primary Wiki navigation. Items correspond to top-level Wiki categories with content and should route to the matching first-level category or section.

typography.label: Desktop: Body/SM, 12px/18px, regular; active text uses var(--color-primary-base), default text uses var(--color-text-weak). Mobile: 14px/20px PingFang SC Bold in the observed SecondaryPage mobile tab rows.

children.rule: Visible tab label for a second-level Wiki navigation branch.

state.rule: Active tab represents the selected second-level branch. Do not use Nav.SecondaryTab for the global first-level top navigation; use Nav.TopBar there.

observedStructure.rule: Vertical local navigation used when a selected second-level tab contains real third-level groups or when a long detail page needs section anchors. In the multi-level secondary template it sits left of the card list and has a right divider, but it remains optional when the source page has only second-level navigation.
observedStructure.mobileRule: Below 600px, do not render the desktop vertical sidebar. For secondary multi-nav pages, use the horizontal mobile local navigation row from node 590:10996: reference x=12, y=200, w=448, h=32; allow horizontal scroll and no wrapping. Mobile document/article pages do not render local anchors.

items.rule: For secondary multi-level pages, items are third-level groups under the active SecondaryTab only when those groups exist in the source page. For detail pages, items are section anchors. Exactly one item should resolve to active at render time.

activeId.rule: Controlled selected item id. When provided, renderer owns the active state and Nav.Navigate only emits onActiveChange.

defaultActiveId.rule: Initial selected item id for uncontrolled usage. If omitted, use the first item marked state=active, otherwise the first item.

onActiveChange.rule: Called with the clicked item id. In uncontrolled usage, clicking updates the internal active item and clears active styling from all other items.

state.rule: Item-level visual state. If an item id matches activeId or internal selected id, active wins. Other items with state=active are normalized to default so multiple active items cannot render at once.

observedStructure.rule: Compact command/link row used in the document-guide related information sidebar. Current guide sidebar item is w=260, h=34, padding follows the Figma component, radius 4, background var(--color-alpha-white-alpha-4), title text one line, optional badge, and right arrow. The text/content region uses fill behavior inside the row: when the component width grows, the content region expands and the trailing badge plus right arrow stay pinned to the right edge instead of floating near the label. Items stack with 8px vertical gap in the guide related-info list. Use only for related-info link rows, not for left article anchor navigation.

children.rule: Visible related-info item label. It stays single-line, ellipsizes inside the fill content region, and must not push the trailing badge or right arrow away from the right edge.

icon.rule: Use variant=leftIcon only when the source related item has a real icon.

showBadge.rule: Set false when the source related item has no badge or status label.

observedStructure.rule: This card is the top-level Wiki category entry on the home page. The circular preview slots summarize up to the first three items of the linked secondary page in display order, and the trailing count shows the total number of items contained by that category. Hide the whole card when the total item count is 0.
observedStructure.hoverRule: Render hover state only when the card can click through to a real secondary page or otherwise trigger new information. If it cannot navigate or trigger a real information change, keep the default state even on pointer hover.
observedStructure.mobile: Below 600px, WikiHome-mobile renders CategoryCard in a single column at w=351, h=114 with 12px row gap. Title uses 16px/24px PingFang SC Bold, description uses 14px/20px PingFang SC Regular, and count badge uses 12px/14px.

imageSrc.rule: Compatibility fallback only. New Wiki mappings should populate avatarSrcs from linked secondary-page items; use imageSrc only when a legacy caller has one approved category-level image and no avatarSrcs.

avatarSrcs.rule: Provide up to three preview asset URLs derived from the first three items of the linked secondary page in display order. Each item may use an icon or image; when both exist, use icon first. Render the assets at the circular preview size. Do not pad with invented assets when fewer than three items exist.

countLabel.rule: Display the total item count contained by the category or linked secondary page, not the number of preview avatars currently shown. If the total count is 0, the renderer should omit this CategoryCard.

state.rule: Use state=hover only for interactive category cards that can click through to a real secondary page or trigger new information. Non-interactive category cards must not display hover styling.

interactive.rule: Set true only when the card has a real target route or action that reveals new information.

observedStructure.itemSizing: Each breadcrumb item hugs its label text width. Do not use the old fixed 94px item width; current component frame height is 26px, text uses 12px font size with 18px line height, and separator spacing follows the component style.

items.rule: Use only on the final detail page, where the current level has no further subcategory page. The number of items equals the page hierarchy, and every breadcrumb item is clickable to its target page.

observedStructure.rule: Reusable image frame. The outer frame follows the selected ratio and caller/page sizing, while the image placement area is inset by 4px on every side to match the Figma component's maximum image slot: Ratio=1:1 uses outer 80x80 with image slot 72x72; Ratio=3:2 uses outer 120x80 with image slot 112x72; Ratio=16:9 uses outer 142x80 with image slot 134x72; Ratio=4:5 uses outer 64x80 with image slot 56x72. Page or parent layout may scale the outer frame, but the component must keep this internal image placement area instead of letting the image cover the full background frame.

ratio.rule: Choose the closest supported ratio from the source asset dimensions or visual content before rendering. Use 1:1 for square icons and near-square assets; use 4:5 for vertical portraits, character/card art, posters, and other portrait assets around width/height 0.8; use 3:2 for moderately wide images; use 16:9 for wide landscape assets, banners, weapon/item renders, maps, screenshots, and other assets that would be noticeably cropped in a square frame. Do not default to 1:1 when source width/height or visual content clearly indicates a vertical or wider image.

fit.rule: Controls how the source image fills the internal image placement area, not the full outer background frame. Use cover by default to preserve existing pages; use contain for weapons, attachments, commodity thumbnails, and item art that must remain fully visible.

observedStructure.rule: Wiki list/detail describe card. Use it when the source entry has richer title, description, image, badge, badge-group detail fields, meta/attribute content, or secondary rich text that needs more than one visible line. The component's own default sizing hugs rendered content; page/template layout requirements for width, height, grid track, or CSS sizing variables take priority when a page explicitly constrains the card. Optional regions are controlled by ShowImage, ShowTitle, ShowDescription, ShowBadge, ShowBadgeGroup, and ShowAttributes; when a region is disabled, do not render it and do not reserve its spacing. For page/list card consistency, if any card in the same rendered page/list needs more than one visible subtitle/body line, use Layout.DescribeCard with size=sm for all related cards on that current page/list instead of Game.ShowCard. Here same rendered page/list means the active secondary tab plus optional active third-level group; do not inherit this decision across sibling second-level tabs or the whole active first-level category. This is separate from the map popup detail card.
observedStructure.smDefaultNode: Current Size=SM examples hug content by default. Use horizontal auto-layout, padding 8, gap 12, 1px white 10% stroke, radius 6. The default content column is 186px wide; the nested Base.Image keeps its aspect ratio with a 100px natural image box by default. Pages may explicitly override the image size variable for larger SM item art, but the image must not stretch just because the card or list row becomes taller. Title/description group uses gap 4; title row is h=22; subtitle text uses Body/SM 12px/18px and wraps to the source content instead of forcing a fixed 36px region. Attribute row uses padding 4px 6px, gap 2.
observedStructure.lgDefaultNode: Current Size=LG examples hug content by default. Use vertical auto-layout, padding top/left/right 8 and bottom 12, gap 8. The default content width is 198px; the nested Base.Image keeps its aspect ratio and horizontally fills that content width. For wide weapon or item art, use imageRatio=3:2 and imageFit=contain when the source image must remain fully visible inside the frame. The card may be constrained by the page template; page-level width/height/grid rules override the component's natural size.
observedStructure.badgeGroupRule: Badge-group is a dedicated optional row between the description block and the attributes/meta row. It uses horizontal layout with 6px item gap and each item is a Base.Badge, typically info-toned when carrying detailed fields such as caliber, slots, or damage.
observedStructure.nestedImageComponent: The image/media region is a nested Base.Image component, not a DescribeCard-owned raw image layer. Render it through ImageFrame/Base.Image and pass imageSrc, imageRatio, and imageFit to that nested component. For Size=SM, keep aspect ratio using the component's natural image box unless the page explicitly overrides the image size; do not stretch the image just because the card row becomes taller. For Size=LG, keep aspect ratio and horizontally fill the image/content width. When showImage=false, remove the nested Base.Image instance and collapse its spacing.
observedStructure.smDescriptionRule: For size=sm, render description/subtitle as its own text node under the title row, not as a one-line ShowCard subtitle. The description region height adapts to the rendered source text using 12px text with 18px line-height. Do not clamp by default; use Base.ToolTip only if a specific template or host container intentionally truncates or hides the text.
observedStructure.lgNoAttributesRule: For size=lg with showAttributes=false, the card height must adapt to rendered content instead of keeping the full LG fixed height reserved for the attributes row.
observedStructure.lgNoDescriptionRule: For size=lg when description/subtitle is absent, render the image-first structure as image plus title and any explicitly bound badge/meta only. Do not render an empty subtitle row or reserve subtitle spacing, and let the card height adapt to image, title, and padding.
observedStructure.hoverRule: Render hover state only when the card can click through, open a detail/modal, expand, play, or otherwise trigger new information. If it is display-only, do not show hover styling.
observedStructure.mobileSm: Below 600px, SecondaryPage-mobile renders Layout.DescribeCard Size=SM as a single-column list: card w=351, h=106 in the observed reference. Title uses 14px/20px PingFang SC Regular, description uses 12px/18px PingFang SC Regular, and badges/meta use 12px/14px.
observedStructure.mobileLg: Below 600px, SecondaryPageLargeCard-mobile renders Layout.DescribeCard Size=LG as a single-column image-first card: card w=351, observed h=312.44 for 16:9 images and h=459 for square image-first items. Content/media width is 335; the observed 16:9 image frame is 335 x 188.44 and square image frame is 335 x 335. Title uses 16px/24px PingFang SC Bold, description uses 14px/20px PingFang SC Regular, and badges use 12px/14px.

title.rule: Required only when showTitle=true. When showTitle=false, do not render the title text or reserve the title row for it.

description.rule: For size=sm, render as the two-line subtitle node observed in Figma node 177:1104: width follows the 210px content column, 12px/18px typography, max visible height 36px. Wrap with Base.ToolTip for the full text only when truncation or hidden text is detected. When absent on size=lg, do not render an empty subtitle row or preserve subtitle spacing.

imageSrc.rule: Forward to the nested Base.Image/ImageFrame component. Do not render a separate raw img layer outside the nested image component.

imageRatio.rule: Bind to the nearest supported ratio for the current rendered page/list. If source dimensions are available, use width/height: near 1.0 -> 1:1, around 0.8 -> 4:5, around 1.5 -> 3:2, around or above 1.7 -> 16:9. If dimensions are unavailable, infer from content type: square icons and near-square art -> 1:1; vertical portraits, character/card art, and posters -> 4:5; weapons, landscape screenshots, map thumbnails, and wide item art -> 16:9 or 3:2. Do not inherit an imageRatio chosen for a sibling second-level tab or another secondary page under the same first-level category.

imageFit.rule: Forward to the nested Base.Image/ImageFrame component. Default cover preserves existing pages. Use contain for weapons, attachments, commodity/item thumbnails, or other asset types where the full image must remain visible inside the DescribeCard image frame.

showImage.rule: When false, do not render the image/media region and let the remaining content fill the card layout.

showTitle.rule: When false, do not render the title text. If showBadge is also false, omit the whole title row.

showDescription.rule: When false, do not render the description/subtitle text and do not reserve subtitle spacing.

showBadge.rule: When false, do not render the badge. If showTitle is also false, omit the whole title row.

showBadgeGroup.rule: When false, do not render the badge-group detail row and do not reserve its spacing.

showAttributes.rule: When false, do not render the attribute/meta row. For size=lg, the overall card height should adapt to title, description, badge, and image content.

size.rule: Use size=sm when replacing Game.ShowCard because any source card in the current rendered page/list needs more than one visible subtitle/body line. This consistency scope is the current page/list only, not the whole first-level category.

state.rule: Use state=hover only when interactive=true or a real click/action target exists. Display-only cards must remain visually default on pointer hover.

interactive.rule: Set true only when the card has a real target route or action that reveals new information.

badges.rule: Legacy single-badge fallback. If badge is absent, the first item may be used as the title-row badge. Do not use this array for the dedicated badge-group row.

badgeGroup.rule: Optional badge-group detail row rendered between description and attributes. Each item may be a plain label or a Base.Badge-like object with tone/variant when the source needs richer field chips.

page.rule: Current page comes from route, query state, or renderer state. Do not use preview defaults in generated output.

totalPages.rule: Total page count must come from backend or standardized datasource pagination metadata.

observedStructure.rule: ShowCard is a compact list/detail summary card for entries whose subtitle/body text fits within one visible line. Use the right arrow only when the represented item has a next page, next hierarchy level, target route, or explicit clickable drill-down. The subtitle/description region clamps to one visible line; full text must be exposed through Base.ToolTip on text hover when truncation is possible. Use variant=voice for source entries whose primary action is playing an audio clip. Do not use ShowCard for the current rendered page/list when its visible source card text requires more than one visible subtitle/body line; use Layout.DescribeCard size=sm for that current page/list instead. Do not apply this decision across sibling second-level tabs or the whole first-level category.
observedStructure.hoverRule: Render hover state only when the card can click through, play audio, open a detail/modal, or otherwise trigger new information. If it only displays static information, do not show hover styling.

voiceVariant.rule: Voice state is an audio playback row: left play/pause icon, title, right time label, and bottom playback progress bar. The play/pause icon uses var(--color-icon-strong); the playback progress fill uses var(--color-primary-base). It represents playable audio content, not drill-down navigation.

observedStructure.mobile: Below 600px, SecondaryPageSimpleInfo-mobile renders Game.ShowCard as a single-column row at w=351, h=76. In SecondaryPageMultiNav-mobile, ShowCard rows may be w=448 inside a horizontally scrollable content area. Title uses 16px/24px PingFang SC Bold, description uses 14px/20px PingFang SC Regular, and badge uses 12px/14px.

description.rule: Render as subtitle/body text. Show at most one visible line and truncate overflow. Preserve the full string and wrap the truncated visible text with Base.ToolTip so hover shows the complete description. If any source description/rich text in the same page/list requires more than one visible line, this is not a ShowCard case; select Layout.DescribeCard size=sm for all related cards in that page/list.

imageRatio.rule: Bind to the nearest supported ratio for the current rendered page/list. Use 1:1 for square icons and near-square item art; use 4:5 for vertical portraits, character/card art, posters, and portrait-like item images; use 3:2 or 16:9 for wide source assets such as weapons, equipment thumbnails, landscapes, maps, screenshots, and banners so the image is not unnecessarily cropped. Do not inherit an imageRatio chosen for a sibling second-level tab or another secondary page under the same first-level category.

progress.rule: Required for variant=process as completion percent, and optional for variant=voice as audio playback progress percent. Do not render fabricated progress defaults in final output.

variant.rule: Use label when title, description, image, and optional badge fully express the source entry. Use process only when the source entry has a real progress value. Use voice when the source entry has playable audio such as voice lines, pronunciation, narration, or sound clips.

audioSrc.rule: Required for production variant=voice. Bind to the playable audio URL or asset id; playback is controlled by renderer state.

currentTimeLabel.rule: Used only for variant=voice. Display current playback time, for example 0:00.

durationLabel.rule: Used only for variant=voice. Display total audio duration, for example 0:04.

isPlaying.rule: Used only for variant=voice. Controls whether the play or pause icon is shown.

targetRoute.rule: Route to the next page or detail level. Presence of a real targetRoute implies the right arrow should be shown unless showRightIcon is explicitly false.

hasNextLevel.rule: Set false when the represented item is terminal and cannot drill down. When false, hide the right arrow even if the card remains selectable for local state.

showRightIcon.rule: Explicit visual override for the right arrow. Default compatibility behavior shows the arrow when neither targetRoute nor hasNextLevel is supplied; final Wiki mappings should set hasNextLevel or targetRoute to avoid ambiguity.

interactive.rule: Set true only when the card has a real target route, drill-down, playable audio action, modal/detail opener, or another action that reveals new information. Non-interactive cards must not display hover styling.

observedStructure.rule: Use together with Game.ProgressBar when source data contains a real progress-like numeric value and label. Do not infer progress from unrelated text.

Use for structured detail content when source sections match one of the supported structures: title/description, features, infoItems, statusBadges, structured tableColumns/tableRows/table, or an approved media/aside block. Do not force unstructured content into this component when the source shape does not match.
observedStructure.compositionRule: A detail page may be assembled from multiple Layout.DetailCard modules. Each module represents one source detail section, and the renderer chooses which optional internal blocks to render from that section's actual data. The only required content for each module is title plus at least one optional content block from description, features, infoItems, statusBadges, tableColumns/tableRows/table, aside, media, or approved child content.
observedStructure.orderingRule: Within a Layout.DetailCard module, keep title first, then render only the optional blocks that exist in the source data, preserving the source section order where possible. Do not force every DetailCard to include all internal parts just because the Figma component demonstrates them.
observedStructure.titleRow: Title renders inside an internal H1 row: horizontal auto layout, h=20, gap=6, vertically centered.
observedStructure.titleDecoration: Always render the internal title decoration before the title text. Figma node Rectangle 1 is a primary-color vertical cut marker, visual size 1.5 x 14, using var(--color-primary-base). This decoration is component chrome and must not be mapped from datasource.
observedStructure.mobile: Below 600px, DetailPageAllStates-mobile uses Layout.DetailCard modules at w=319 inside the 351px detail content shell, with content-driven heights such as 84, 128, 258, or 232. Do not preserve desktop 928px module width on mobile.

tableColumns.rule: Column definitions come from the source table schema. Use these columns to set the table grid template; do not hardcode a fixed number of columns. Detail tables should use compressible column tracks such as minmax(0, 1fr), follow the parent container width, and must not set a fixed 928px table width or horizontal scrolling for wide tables such as character intimacy tables.

tableRows.rule: Rows come from the source table data and are rendered by repeating Data.TableRowCell across each source row and column.

observedStructure.rule: This is a reusable table header cell. It does not define the whole table, row count, or column count. Repeat one header cell per source column.
observedStructure.mobile: Below 600px, repeat header cells inside the mobile table width, typically inside a 319px Layout.DetailCard. Use compressible column tracks and do not preserve desktop fixed table widths.

observedStructure.rule: This is a reusable table body cell. It does not define the whole table, row count, or column count. Repeat one row cell per source row and column. Use state=none only for intentionally empty or unavailable table cells. Text wraps with overflow-wrap:anywhere rather than nowrap or ellipsis.
observedStructure.mobile: Below 600px, row cells must fit the mobile table width, typically inside a 319px Layout.DetailCard. Keep 12px/18px text, allow wrapping, and avoid fixed desktop table width.

observedUse: any component text that is intentionally truncated and needs hover full-text disclosure

observedStructure.rule: Text hover disclosure bubble for truncated or visually hidden text only. Do not show a tooltip when the wrapped text is fully visible and not clipped. Do not use this component for map markers; Map.MapTip remains the map point marker.
observedStructure.trigger: Wrap the visible text node that owns the clamp/ellipsis/hidden styling. The trigger keeps its original layout and text clamp rules.
observedStructure.bubble: Background color/bg/weak, radius lg, horizontal padding 12px, vertical padding 4px, Body/MD 14px/20px text in color/text/strong, regular-small white drop shadow.
observedStructure.tail: 12x6 triangle tail attached to the bubble edge. Supported directions place the tail at TopLeft, TopCenter, TopRight, BottomLeft, BottomCenter, BottomRight, Left, or Right.
observedStructure.behavior: Hidden by default. Enable display only when the wrapped text is actually truncated, overflow-clipped, or visually hidden while the full text exists. Show on mouse hover over the wrapped text and on keyboard focus of the parent interactive card when applicable, after a short delay of about 120ms. The bubble is non-interactive and must not affect layout size.
observedStructure.portalRule: The visible bubble must render in a page/body-level overlay or portal, not as a normal child inside the hovered text/card/list container. The trigger remains inside the original component for measurement, but the bubble must escape overflow:hidden, clip, and scroll containers so it is never cropped by small cards or list rows. A tooltip implementation that depends on local overflow:visible, local z-index stacking, or absolute positioning inside the hovered container is invalid.

content.rule: Must contain the complete text, not the visually truncated string.

children.rule: Keep the original text component and clamp/ellipsis/hidden styles inside ToolTip so the component can detect whether the visible text is clipped before showing the bubble.

placement.rule: Use auto for production text disclosure so the component chooses the direction that best fits the current viewport. Do not constrain placement to the nearest scroll/clipping container; the bubble is rendered through the portal/overlay specifically to escape those containers. Do not keep the bubble inside the hovered card/list row just to satisfy local layout rules. Use a fixed direction only for intentional component demos or when the caller has already resolved placement.

active.rule: Use auto by default so ToolTip only appears when the wrapped text is actually truncated or hidden. Use true only when the renderer already knows the text is hidden/clipped and auto measurement cannot detect it.

showDelayMs.rule: Short hover delay before showing the bubble. Keep around 120ms unless a design update specifies another timing.

observedUse: source pages that explain a single item through a modal overlay

observedStructure.rule: Centered modal for item explanation content only. Use this component when the original source data/page represents the selected item explanation as a modal, not as a normal detail page, tooltip, card expansion, or map popup.
observedStructure.overlay: Place over the current page with a #000000 overlay at 50% opacity. The page underneath remains visible only through the dim layer.
observedStructure.dialog: Base / Modal / Default component set: Property=Detail node 436:5415 is 381 x 484; Property=Vedio node 438:3662 is 381 x 290. Center the panel above the overlay, using the selected variant height.
observedStructure.chrome: Panel background #11161B, radius 12, no outer stroke. Close button is x=341 y=12, 28 x 28, radius 6, fill #FFFFFF @ 12%, icon #7E8994.
observedStructure.close: Close icon button at the top-right of the panel. It closes the modal and returns to the underlying page state.
observedStructure.detailVariant: Property=Detail: optional 100x100 centered image, centered title 16px/24px bold #F5F7FA, optional subtitle 12px/18px #AEB6BE, and lower 357 x 256 description box. Omit unavailable optional fields and collapse their spacing.
observedStructure.videoVariant: Property=Vedio in Figma, exposed as variant=video in code: panel 381 x 290 with a 357 x 238 video area at x=12, y=40 and a centered play icon. Use for video source material only.
observedStructure.description: Detail variant lower description box is the max-visible text area: padding 8, 1px #FFFFFF @ 10% stroke, radius 8, text 14px/20px #AEB6BE. It grows with content until its max height, then only this description area scrolls vertically with the Layout.Scroll visual style: 12px track, 4px thumb, #FFFFFF @ 12%.
observedStructure.mobileDetail: Below 600px, mobile detail modal follows node 624:7298: overlay over the viewport, panel x=20, y=210, w=335, h=464 on the 375px reference. Detail image uses Base.Image Ratio=1:1 at 80x80. Description remains the internal scroll area. Mobile video modal is not defined yet and remains TODO_FROM_FIGMA_OR_IMPLEMENTATION.

variant.rule: Use detail for item text/image explanation modals. Use video only when the modal source is video media. The Figma variant is named Property=Vedio, but code uses the corrected value video.

title.rule: Required for variant=detail unless the source modal intentionally omits a title. Not required for variant=video.

subtitle.rule: Bind the item's brief summary when present; omit the row when absent.

description.rule: Bind the full explanatory text. Do not truncate the source string; if it exceeds the description box max height, scroll inside the description area.

imageSrc.rule: Bind the modal item's icon/image when present. The observed state uses a 100x100 centered square image.

videoSrc.rule: Used only for variant=video. Bind the video source URL or playable media asset. Provide videoSrc or posterSrc when rendering the video variant.

posterSrc.rule: Used only for variant=video. Bind the video poster/thumbnail when available; it may be shown before playback or as a fallback when videoSrc is unavailable.

videoTitle.rule: Accessible title for the video source material.

videoControls.rule: Enable native controls for playable video unless the host renderer supplies a separate player control layer.

onClose.rule: Close the modal and restore the underlying page state.

maxHeight.rule: Defaults to 484 for variant=detail and 290 for variant=video. Height remains content-adaptive below the active variant cap when applicable.

descriptionMaxHeight.rule: Maximum height of the lower explanation area. When text exceeds this area, show the internal vertical scrollbar using the Layout.Scroll visual style.

mode.rule: Use fixed in production pages. Inline is only for component gallery or isolated template previews.

showOverlay.rule: Keep true for source-page modal usage so the 50% black dim layer is rendered.

observedStructure.positioning: x/y coordinates anchor the bottom-center tail tip to the map point inside the pannable and zoomable map coordinate system.
observedStructure.zoomRule: MapTip marker visual size stays constant during map zoom; the map canvas renderer recalculates screen x/y from map coordinates and zoom.
observedStructure.rule: This component represents a map point marker, not a visible text bubble. Use Base.ToolTip for truncated text hover disclosure.
observedStructure.layerRule: MapTip markers must stay below Map.PopupDescribeCard when they overlap; PopupDescribeCard is the active map detail surface.
observedStructure.mobile: Default marker 32x38 in default/collapsed/expanded/search states; selected marker may be 40x46 at x=163,y=315 in MobileMapMarkerSelectedScreen.

content.rule: Used as point label/accessibility text and short point info; not rendered as visible text inside MapTip.

icon.rule: Use point/category icon when datasource provides one; otherwise renderer may use a neutral map marker icon.

selected.rule: When true, renderer may use the observed selected marker visual state while preserving map-coordinate anchoring.

observedStructure.layoutRule: When imageSrc is absent, card height must adapt to title and description content instead of keeping the full image-card height.
observedStructure.closeButton: Top-right close button default state has transparent background; hover/focus state shows the white alpha background.
observedStructure.noMediaTitleRow: When imageSrc and description are both absent, title and close button must align on the same visual row; close top offset is adjusted only for the no-image state.
observedStructure.layerRule: DescribeCard must render above MapTip markers in map.canvas.
observedStructure.mobileRule: Do not use the desktop fixed lower-right 280x266 position on mobile. Use mobile.map.detail when specified.
observedStructure.mobileSelected: 343x86 at x=16, y=602 in MobileMapMarkerSelectedScreen; render only for selected marker detail and hide BottomSheet while open.
observedStructure.mobileCloseRule: When the mobile detail card close action fires, clear selectedMarkerId and restore the previous BottomSheet state instead of staying in markerSelected.
